headlines.title: Japan chipmaker Kioxia FY 2025 net profit doubled to 554.49 bil. yen
Tokyo stocks lose ground after early gains
Japan stocks lower at close of trade; Nikkei 225 down 1.76%
Japan Stocks End The Week Split As Tech Profits Fade
BOJ warns of financial system risks from investment fund activity
European stocks set to fall as inflation fears return; Starmer faces leadership challenge
Alphabet (GOOGL) Makes History with Record Yen Bond Sale in Japan — And It’s All to Pay for AI

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 장 초반 상승분을 반납하며 하락 마감했으며, 닛케이 225 지수는 1.76% 하락하며 한 주를 혼조세로 마쳤습니다. 개별 종목으로는 반도체 기업 키옥시아가 2025 회계연도 순이익 두 배 증가를 발표했으나, 전반적인 기술주 이익은 둔화되는 모습을 보였습니다. 한편, 알파벳이 AI 투자 자금 조달을 위해 일본에서 엔화 채권을 사상 최대 규모로 발행했으며, 일본은행은 투자 펀드 활동으로 인한 금융 시스템 리스크를 경고했습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese stocks closed lower today, with the Nikkei 225 falling 1.76%, reversing earlier gains and ending the week on a mixed note. While chipmaker Kioxia reported a significant profit increase, broader tech profits appeared to fade, contributing to the market's split performance. Separately, Alphabet made a historic yen bond sale in Japan to fund AI initiatives, even as the Bank of Japan warned of potential financial system risks from investment fund activity.
